question_text: Two springs are tested. Spring A has a spring constant of 100 N/m and Spring B has a spring constant of 50 N/m. When the same force is applied to both springs, which statement is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: Spring A stretches more because it has a larger spring constant

options.label: B
options.text: Spring B stretches more because it has a smaller spring constant

options.label: C
options.text: Both springs stretch by the same amount

options.label: D
options.text: Spring A stretches more because it is stiffer

correct_answer: Spring B stretches more because it has a smaller spring constant
explanation: Hooke's law states that F = k × e, where F is the force, k is the spring constant, and e is the extension. If the same force is applied to both springs, a smaller spring constant means a larger extension. Spring B has a smaller spring constant (50 N/m compared to 100 N/m), so it is less stiff and will stretch more than Spring A.
